# `results/` — numerical results regenerated by the experiments

Every numerical claim in the paper is emitted by a script in
[`../notebooks/`](../notebooks/) as a small JSON file in this directory.
The JSON files *are* tracked in git so that the headline numbers can
be cited at the commit level; the per-script intermediate `.npz`
arrays and per-checkpoint `.trajectories.pkl` files are excluded
(see `.gitignore`) because they are regeneratable from the source code
in seconds and are too large for the repository.

## Expected layout after a clean run

```
results/
├── stp_identity_residuals.json         # §4   from notebooks/stp_loss/
├── descriptive_z_scores.json           # §5   from notebooks/stp_loss/
├── pythia_replication.json             # §6   from notebooks/cross_model/
├── R2_three_way_separator.json         # §8   from notebooks/conservative_arch/run_full_pipeline.py
├── R2_oracle.json                      # §9.1 from notebooks/conservative_arch/splm_oracle_fit.py
├── R2_capacity_sweep.json              # §9.2 from notebooks/conservative_arch/sharedV_capacity_sweep.py
├── R2_token_direction.json             # §9.3 from notebooks/conservative_arch/token_direction_fit.py
├── jacobian_symmetry_pca16.json        # §7.2 from notebooks/conservative_arch/jacobian_symmetry.py
└── leakfree_rerun_R2.json              # §A.3 from notebooks/conservative_arch/ln_damping_sweep/analyse_sweep.py
```

## Robustness extensions (not paper-headline numbers)

Robustness extensions and post-submission diagnostic sweeps live under their own subdirectory and follow the conventions of the extension's own README rather than the headline-JSON pattern above.

- [`pca_sweep/`](./pca_sweep/) — executed-2026-05-17 §5.1 PCA-symmetry sweep artefacts (PCA-16 vs PCA-32 on GPT-2 small and Pythia-160M). Backs the "Robustness under the PCA-dimension choice" footnote in `paper_tmlr_1` §7.2 and the §4.2 falsification block of the `q9e_n` verdict note in the `semsimula` repo. **Verdict: REJECTED** (PCA-16 finding is robust at PCA-32 on both architectures; max gaps 0.089 / 0.067 < 0.10 threshold).

## Provenance header

Each JSON file begins with a provenance block recording:

```json
{
  "_provenance": {
    "git_commit":   "<40-char SHA>",
    "code_path":    "notebooks/.../<script>.py",
    "config_hash":  "<sha256 of resolved config>",
    "checkpoint_sha256": "<sha256 of consumed .pt, if any>",
    "random_seed":  0,
    "timestamp":    "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ",
    "host":         "<machine identifier>"
  },
  "...": "..."
}
```

This allows any cited number to be traced back to the exact script
revision, configuration, and checkpoint that produced it.
